DRP_NAME: Autopoietic Cryptographic Forging: IAG Invariant Map

Stage_0_Baseline_Manifold.description: Continuous profiling of the legacy system's nominal execution manifold M_nominal
Stage_0_Baseline_Manifold.computational_complexity: O(n^3) for Vietoris-Rips complex, approximated O(n log n) via GUDHI landmark sampling
Stage_0_Baseline_Manifold.implementation_note: Use GUDHI or Ripser++ for incremental persistent homology; cache barcode fingerprints as BLAKE3 hashes

Stage_1_Entropy_Capture.description: Real-time entropy measurement of anomalous execution path delta against M_nominal
Stage_1_Entropy_Capture.empirical_reference: HYDRA/RAVEN eBPF ring-buffer sampling at 1:1000 events, arXiv:2510.03712

Stage_2_TDA_Embedding.description: Lift execution delta vector into persistent homology space; compute Betti signature of the anomalous path
Stage_2_TDA_Embedding.method: Vietoris-Rips filtration over point cloud formed by (PC, SP, IP) register triplets at each syscall boundary
Stage_2_TDA_Embedding.critical_invariant: beta_1 count indicating newly traversed 1-cycles (topological holes) not present in M_nominal
Stage_2_TDA_Embedding.NP_hardness_mitigation: Restrict TDA to bounded-dimension d<=3 simplicial complex; use spectral gap approximation (arXiv:2506.01432)
Stage_2_TDA_Embedding.empirical_reference: PhoGAD graph-based persistent homology, arXiv:2401.10547; PHANTOM system, arXiv:2312.00023

Stage_3_IAG_Inversion.description: Inverted Abductive Grounding: compute the minimal geometric constraint C* that renders the attack path topologically infeasible

formal_definition.objective: Find invariant constraint C* = argmin_{C in Constraints} |C| s.t. H_1(M_nominal UNION path_attack | C) = 0
formal_definition.interpretation: C* is the minimal additional boundary condition that collapses the beta_1 hole the exploit traversed
formal_definition.abductive_logic: Standard abduction: P(cause|effect). IAG: Find C such that P(attack_path | M_nominal UNION C) -> 0
formal_definition.IAG_loss_function: L_IAG(C) = D_KL(P_attack || P_benign | C) - lambda * |C|  (maximize separation, minimize invariant size)

Stage_3_IAG_Inversion.ZKP_circuit_type: Groth16 / PLONK over BN254 curve for O(1) verification by legacy verifier
Stage_3_IAG_Inversion.failure_mode: Semantic Saponification: C* too broad -> API freeze. Guard: Jaccard similarity J(C*, M_nominal_allowed_paths) > 0.92
Stage_3_IAG_Inversion.empirical_reference: ZKP NTT latency analysis arXiv:2509.22684; ZKP applications survey arXiv:2408.00243

Stage_4_z0star_Quench.description: Thermodynamic solidification: fuse C* into the z0* Constitutional Austenite base invariant layer
Stage_4_z0star_Quench.metallurgical_analogy: Martensite quench: high-entropy attack state provides activation energy E_a to overcome transition barrier Delta_G

Hamiltonian_formulation.alpha: Attack entropy weight: alpha = H_delta / H_max_observed
Hamiltonian_formulation.Phi: 1.6180339887 (Golden Ratio density constraint to prevent resonance)
Hamiltonian_formulation.stability_condition: Tr(Hess(H_quench)) < kappa_max (bounded Hessian trace ensures non-resonance)
Hamiltonian_formulation.catastrophic_forgetting_guard: EWC-style elastic weight penalty on existing z0* invariants with lambda_ewc = 1e4

Stage_5_eBPF_Compilation.description: Translate continuous topological invariant C* into discrete eBPF/XDP bytecode for legacy mainframe network edge
Stage_5_eBPF_Compilation.pluriversal_bridge: Continuous AI geometry -> Discrete kernel enforcement. Legacy COBOL sees only O(1) hash verification.
compilation_steps: 1. Serialize C* as a deterministic predicate over (src_ip, dst_port, payload_entropy, syscall_sequence_hash)
2. Compile predicate to eBPF bytecode via LLVM BPF backend or custom Rust no_std eBPF library
3. Sign bytecode with Ed25519 key from Hardware Security Module (HSM)
4. Load via bpf() syscall at XDP hook; attach to mainframe network edge via tc/BPF_PROG_TYPE_SK_MSG
5. Mainframe verifies Ed25519 signature in O(1) before accepting any state transition
Stage_5_eBPF_Compilation.legacy_compatibility: IBM Z-Series: eBPF enforcement at OSA-Express network adapter layer; COBOL program untouched
Stage_5_eBPF_Compilation.discretization_loss_guard: Empirically test: inject original exploit post-eBPF; if any path succeeds, flag Pluriversal Bridge Falsification
Stage_5_eBPF_Compilation.empirical_reference: eBPF BPF-LSM Google KRSI, eunomia.dev 2025; eBPF Foundation 2025 Year in Review

Failure_Modes.FM1_Semantic_Saponification: C* so broad it blocks all traffic. Guard: coverage ratio check.
Failure_Modes.FM2_Austenite_Shatter: IAG misclassifies flash-crash as zero-day. Guard: Black Swan market event oracle feed.
Failure_Modes.FM3_Pluriversal_Bridge_Falsification: Exploit bypasses discrete eBPF approximation. Guard: empirical post-deployment penetration test.
Failure_Modes.FM4_Adversarial_Invariant_Poisoning: Attacker uses sacrificial exploits to forge invariants blocking legitimate flows. Guard: rate-limit invariant forging to max 3 per 24h window.

SELF_TEST_Metrics.M1_Autopoietic_Autonomy: Human heuristic rules = 0 in steady state post-bootstrap
SELF_TEST_Metrics.M2_Legacy_Compatibility: COBOL program diff = 0 bytes; eBPF module deployed at edge only
SELF_TEST_Metrics.M3_Latency_Bound: End-to-end IAG->Quench->eBPF < 50ms on commodity hardware (16-core Xeon, 64GB RAM)
